title: Red Baraat in the Amphitheatre
description:
The "infectious" (Rhein Main Press), "cacophonous bhangra funk band from Brooklyn" (The Telegraph) is “the best party band in years” (NPR), and they're coming to Montclair for a free outdoor performance in the Amphitheatre! Join PEAK Performances and Red Baraat for a spring celebration of the year with free ice cream and other free things for Montclair students. 
 Red Baraat  is a pioneering band from Brooklyn, New York. Conceived by dhol player Sunny Jain, the group has drawn worldwide praise for its singular sound, a merging of hard driving Punjabi rhythms with elements of hip-hop, jazz and raw punk energy. The band’s live shows are an unparalleled collision of virtuosity, party, and joy, as Red Baraat reminds us that we are truly all in this together. Red Baraat has toured the globe the past 16 years with stops at Bonnaroo, Austin City Limits, Bumbershoot, New Orleans Jazz Fest, Peter Gabriel’s WOMAD festivals (Australia, New Zealand, Spain, UK), Dubai World Expo 2021, just to name a few. Along the way they sold out rooms as diverse as the Luxembourg Philharmonic and the New York City’s legendary rock club Bowery Ballroom, and performed at the request of The White House (Obama), TED, London Olympic Games, David Byrne, Padma Lakshmi and recent NYC Mayor-elect, Zohran Mamdani. Red Baraat have released 7 albums with numerous accolades from major media outlets and ranked in top 10 lists on Billboard, Apple Music and World Music Charts Europe.

title: BA Visual Arts Thesis Exhibition
description:
Celebrate the end of the semester with this brilliant showcase from our rising Visual Arts graduates! This exhibition marks the culmination of their degree, launching their creative journeys. Each student presents a cohesive body of artwork in the Finley Galleries, our final exhibition of the Spring 2026 semester. 
 Made to be Seen  features an impressive range of interdisciplinary approaches: mixed-media sculpture, large-scale painting, hand-sewn textiles, ceramics, video projection, photography, and installations. Over 30 Visual Arts graduates invite you to explore their creative research and current perspectives, on view through these vibrant capstone projects!   
   
 Exhibiting Artists: Section 1: Veronica Avignone, Christian Campoverde, Mae Elkovich, Lisette Flor, Emily Garces, Aj Malabunga, Jenna Mcdonough, Melanie Medina, Angeline Ortiz, Tasir Plant 
 Section 2: Mari Ahmed, Chez Amos, Kaila Craig, Amanda Lerner, Jeremiah Molin, Kevin Pena, Rose Perez Cruz, Emmanuella Ponzio, Daniel Portillo, Liliana Salce, Giacomo Valerin, Beyza Yildiz 
 Section 3: Duy-Minh Do, Catalina Ferraro, Keaoh Gonzalez, Alexa Grasso, Katelin Guaman, Paulina Guerra, Keita Mcfarlane, Teiona Mcnair, Luz Plata, Diana Valdes, Lloyd Zheng 
   
 Made to be Seen BA Visual Arts Thesis Exhibition Finley Gallery, Finley Hall Exhibition dates: April 23-29, 2026 Closing Reception: Wednesday April 29, 6-8pm

title: A Moment in Time Annual Fashion Show
description:
The Montclair State University Fashion Design and Merchandising program is preparing to unveil its highly anticipated annual juried fashion show, titled  “A Moment in Time.”  Making its way to the runway this week, the event serves as a premier showcase for the technical skill and artistic vision of students across all levels of the program. 
   
 Join us for Montclair State University’s annual fashion show where creativity, experimentation, and serious talent take center stage. From first stitches to advanced draping, this show brings together designers at every level pushing boundaries and redefining what fashion can be. 
   
 Expect bold looks, unexpected silhouettes, and a runway full of moments you’ll want to pause and take in. Think distortion, discovery, and pure creative energy. 
   
 👗 Featuring work from: Intro to Apparel Construction Intermediate Apparel Construction Flat Pattern Draping 
 and Independent Designers 
   
 🧥 Special collab with Rocky’s Closet showing how you can serve looks without breaking the bank 
   
 🏆 Judged by industry pros + scholarships awarded 
   
 📍 Memorial Auditorium 🗓 Friday, April 24 🚪 Doors 5:30 PM | Show 6 PM 
 🎟 $12 presale | $15 at the door 
   
 Grab your tickets now:  https://engagement.montclair.edu/e/montclair-state-university-2026-fashion-show/ 
 Or Scan QR code   
   
 Don’t blink… you might miss the moment 👀

title: Rise Up: Overcoming Challenges and Finding Inner Strength - The show
organization: Center for Latino Heritage & Spanish Language Excellence
description:
"Rise Up" is a collaborative production presented by the  Center for Latino heritage and Spanish language Excellence (CLaSE)  and the  Department of Theater and Dance  at  Montclair State University,  in partnership with the  Montclair Fund for Educational Excellence (MFEE)  and  Dr. Lisa Brenner, Theatre & Dance Department at  Drew University.   
 This showcase features Latino students aged 12 to 17, MSU students and members of our Kichwa community,  primarily from Central and South America, who have developed   original works to express their feelings about their culture and the obstacles they have faced. The performance includes a diverse array of talents, from monologues detailing personal histories in countries like Honduras, Mexico, Guatemala, etc., to choreographed dances and original songs performed in both English and Spanish.  
 The "Rise Up" production serves as a vital bridge between young Latino performers and the university communities. By providing a professional stage for these students to share their narratives of resilience and heritage during the Spring 2026 season, the project validates their artistic capabilities and reinforces their inner strength. This collaboration ensures that student voices are not merely heard but celebrated, fostering a deeper mutual understanding and a lasting connection within the community. 
 Come to support these young talents and enjoy the show!

title: Denim Day 2026: Mind the Gaps, Move Forward
description:
April is Sexual Assault Awareness Month, and together, we can break the silence.  The Montclair State University Police Department and the Office of Advocacy & Outreach are committed to fostering a campus where survivors feel heard, supported, and empowered.  It starts with believing. It starts with you.  By offering a safe and affirming response to those who disclose gender-based and sexual violence, we can create lasting change. Let’s stand together to end the cycle of silence—because  every voice matters, and every action counts. 
 Denim Day is recognized internationally as a day of action and awareness, encouraging people to wear denim to challenge victim-blaming and educate others about sexual violence.  This year, Denim Day falls on Wednesday, April 29, 2026. 
 The University Police and  Office of Advocacy & Outreach and the Office of Advocacy and Community Resilience , in partnership with over 30 campus and local community organizations, invites you to join us  from 9 a.m. to 12 p.m. at the Amphitheater  for a powerful awareness event honoring survivors and increasing awareness of available resources. 
 Despite the  gaps  that still exist in the criminal justice system, in resources, and in prevention education,  we will move forward together  as a community. As part of this commitment, we will walk  one mile around campus in solidarity with survivors.

title: Campus Earth Month
description:
Participate in Campus Earth Month throughout April by collecting code words and earning entries to win prizes. Each code word you collect or action you complete will earn an entry into the Campus Earth Month Raffle. The more entries you earn, the better your odds. 
 Each decision you make has the potential to be good for the planet and people. No one can do it all, but we can all do something. This April, make sure your ledger is balanced so you are always…+1 for the Planet, People, Yourself, & Montclair! 
 Your goal is to collect as many code words as you can throughout April. At every in-person Earth Month event, a code word will be available. It will either be displayed at the event or communicated to you during the event verbally. When the event concludes, you can also ask the organizer for the code word. 
 Sustainability Student Interns will be out on campus with opportunities to earn additional code words and code words will also be available virtually. 
 You are responsible for keeping track of your code words while participating and  submitting them all in one email to   sustainability@montclair.edu  by April, 30 2026. 
 Each decision you make has the potential to be good for the planet. No one can do it all, but we can all do something. This April, make sure your ledger is balanced so you are always…+1 for the Planet, People, Yourself, & Montclair! ❤️🌎😊🦅🐻 Full details, including additional Raffle entry options, a complete prize list, & rules are available at: https://www.montclair.edu/sustainability/earth-month/ For a full-list of events visit here: https://www.montclair.edu/sustainability/earth-month-events/

title: Visit the University Galleries' Spring 2026 Exhibition! Carpe Diem: Select Alumni in the Visual Arts
location: George Segal Gallery, 4th Floor of the Red Hawk Deck
description:
On view from February 3 – May 3, 2026 in the George Segal Gallery. Public hours are Tuesday – Friday, 12 to 4 pm. 
   
 Admission is free, open to the public.  Learn more about this exhibition 
   
 Carpe Diem: Select Alumni in the Visual Arts  is a group exhibition presented by the Office for Development and Alumni Engagement and hosted by the University Galleries.  Carpe Diem  brings together twelve accomplished alumni working in a range of media. 
   
 Curated by Sally Morgan Lehman, who has taught in the Department of Art and Design at Montclair and is owner and director of Morgan Lehman Gallery in New York City,  Carpe Diem  highlights the breadth of artistic practices fostered at Montclair and the lasting impact of its visual arts alumni. 
   
 Featured artists include Flávia Berindoague ’13, Bisa Butler ’05, Jonathan Calm ’97, Kyle Coniglio ’10, Alyssa Fanning ’12, Allen Ginsberg (1943), Márta Kucsora (2005–06), Michael Manning ’11, Nancy Bergman Pantirer ’80, Tiffany Perez ’09, William Pope.L ’78, and Maria Valdivia ’24.

title: Express Yourself! A Day of Creativity, Community and Discovery
organization: College of Humanities and Social Sciences (CHSS)
description:
Step into a world of creativity, movement and shared ideas at Express Yourself!, a vibrant day of arts, culture and creative exploration taking place on Saturday, May 9, in partnership with the Montclair Literary Festival. Open to students, faculty and the wider community, this celebration invites everyone to explore creative expression in many forms. 
 Join us for hands-on workshops and interactive experiences on  Saturday, May 9, 2026 . All events are open to all and free, but registration is required. Sign up for a single workshop or all six! 
 Register to Attend 
 Acting Out: Improv Group Style 9 -10:15 a.m., Life Hall room 1200 Crowd Source Acting is about using improvisation to act in the moment. Participants will be guided in an improvisational game that allows them to experience dropping into a roll and having the inspiration of those around them to achieve a common goal. That goal could be silly, serious, or absurd. Join us for a round or two of a game called Family Portraits, guided by Theatre department practitioners. 
 A Moving Experience For All 10:30-11:45 a.m., Life Hall 1210 This session will be a dance class for beginners, emphasizing freedom of movement and some basic dance moves. 
 Got Art? Take This Workshop 12:00-1:15 p.m., Calcia Hall 245 Participants will explore a visual arts technique such as collage, clay sculpting, or mixed media and create a unique work. Guided by an artist educator, attendees will learn simple methods and experiment with materials during the session. 
 Snap Snap: Taking Just the Right Picture 1:30-2:45 p.m., Feliciano School of Business Lawn This digital photography workshop will start with a presentation on the powerful choices available for capturing and documenting the world with your camera. Each participant will be given directions for taking a photograph from a specific location on campus. Jenelle Covino, Adjunct Professor of Art and Design, will guide participants to utilize basic photo retouching principles to bring each digital photograph to life. You can bring your digital camera or use your cell phone to photograph for this workshop. This workshop is suitable for all ages and all experience levels of photography. 
 Drumming Up a Crowd, the West African Way 3:00-4:15 p.m.; Feliciano School of Business Lawn The Montclair State University West African Drumming and Dance Ensemble will perform and engage everyone to participate in traditional West African recreational music. We play a strong weave of polyrhythms on traditional drums, bells and rattles, and those in attendance will drum with us, sing with us in call and response in a West African language, and dance traditional movements with us, directed by calls from the lead drum. 
 Let’s Get Physical: Writing Poetry about Memory and the Body 4:30-5:45 p.m. Feliciano School of Business Room 140 In this generative poetry workshop, we will begin by bringing our memories to life on the page. We will then expand to writing about embodied experiences. Participants will leave this workshop having written two new poems and having received feedback on one of the poems. They will also leave with a few additional tools in their writers’ toolbox.

title: Safe Space Training
description:
Our Safe Space Training is an intensive, interactive training that provides attendees with information and tools to combat homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, cissexism and heterosexism, on campus and beyond. Through interactive activities and discussion, participants will personally reflect on their own experiences to enhance their personal and professional development, provide further education, and finally, identify ways to create change on campus and beyond. 
 All sessions are three hours and are first-come, first-served. Pre-registration is required. Registrants are expected to attend the full session. Only individuals who have fully completed the Safe Space Program and have agreed to the expectations of its membership are able to display the sticker/button of our program. Refreshments are not provided; however, you are more than welcome to bring your lunch, coffee, snacks, etc.
